radio check: The procedure for a radio check is: 1. Call the receiving station signal. 2. Identify yourself. 3. Say 'Radio Check'. 4. Wait for signal strength and readability report.
salute report: A SALUTE report stands for: Size, Activity, Location, Unit or Uniform, Time, and Equipment. It is used to quickly report enemy sightings.
medical evacuation: The standard 9-Line MEDEVAC requests medical evacuation. Line 1 is Location, Line 2 is Radio Frequency, Line 3 is Number of Patients by Precedence.
fire discipline: Fire discipline is the control of weapon fire by a commander. It ensures ammunition is conserved and targets are engaged effectively based on commands.
navigation protocol: When navigating without GPS: 1. Orient your map to north using a compass. 2. Identify your current location via terrain association. 3. Maintain a strict pace count to track distance traveled.
